pick.rationale: Houston enters as an overwhelming favorite in this 2/15 seed matchup, ranking #5 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite 28-5 record from the Big 12. The efficiency gap is staggering, with Houston's +33.55 adjusted efficiency margin dwarfing Idaho's +1.54, reflecting a massive talent and competition level disparity between the national championship contender and the Big Sky representative. Houston's elite defense ranked 4th nationally should completely stifle Idaho's below-average offense, while the Cougars' tournament experience under Kelvin Sampson provides a decisive edge in this Round of 64 contest.

reasoningStep.title: Houston vs Idaho Efficiency and Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Houston dominates Idaho across all efficiency metrics with a ~32-point adjusted efficiency margin gap, elite top-5 national ranking, and Big 12 pedigree versus Idaho's mid-100s ranking and Big Sky conference tournament win.
reasoningStep.evidence: Houston ranks #5 in both KenPom and Torvik with 28-5 record, +33.55 adjEM, top-15 offense (124.6 adjO), and elite defense (91.0 adjD, 4th in Torvik). Idaho ranks 136th-145th with 21-14 record, +1.54 adjEM, 166th-176th offense, and 122nd defense. Historical 2/15 seed matchups favor 2-seeds at 95%+ win rate. Similar 2/15 picks in this bracket (Duke 95%, Arizona 94%, Florida 94%, Purdue 90%, UConn 88%) support high confidence in the favorite.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

modelTrace.content:
The web searches are consistently failing. However, I have sufficient data from the ratings lookup to make a confident pick. Let me analyze what I have:

Houston:
- Ranked #5 in both Torvik and KenPom
- 28-5 record from Big 12
- Elite defense (4th in Torvik adjDE, 91.0 adjD in KenPom)
- Top-15 offense (13th Torvik adjOE, 16th KenPom adjO)
- +33.55 adjusted efficiency margin (KenPom)

Idaho:
- Ranked 136th (Torvik) / 145th (KenPom)
- 21-14 record from Big Sky
- Below average offense (166th Torvik, 176th KenPom)
- Below average defense (122nd Torvik, ~107th KenPom)
- +1.54 adjusted efficiency margin (KenPom)

This is a massive gap of approximately 32 points in adjusted efficiency margin. Houston is a legitimate national championship contender while Idaho is a mid-major conference tournament winner with a losing-ish record (21-14).

Historical context: 2-seeds win approximately 95%+ of 2/15 matchups. The only time 15-seeds have beaten 2-seeds was when UMBC beat Virginia in 2018, and a few other rare occasions.

Without any injury news (searches failed), I have to assume both teams are at full strength. Given the enormous talent and efficiency gap, Houston should be an overwhelming favorite.

Looking at prior picks in this bracket, similar 2/15 matchups have been picked with very high confidence:
- UConn over Furman (r64-east-8): 88% confidence
- Purdue over Queens (r64-west-8): 90% confidence
- Duke over Siena (r64-east-1): 95% confidence
- Arizona over LIU (r64-west-1): 94% confidence
- Florida over Lehigh (r64-south-1): 94% confidence

Houston vs Idaho appears to be a similar mismatch. Houston at #5 nationally is actually ranked higher than some of those other 2-seeds (UConn, Purdue weren't specified but Houston is top-5). This should be a very high confidence pick.

I'll pick Houston with high confidence (around 92-94% range, similar to other dominant 2/15 matchups in this bracket).
